etymology.description: 從(從) (cóng, "follow; from") combined with 到(到) (dào, "arrive; to") creates a phrase that indicates a span or movement from one point to another.
etymology.interpretation: 從(從) (cóng, "follow; from") indicates the starting point or origin of a movement or action. 到(到) (dào, "arrive; to") signifies the end or destination point. Together, this structure describes a range or transition, illustrating the path or timeline from one point (從) to another (到), effectively mapping out a continuous trajectory or sequence.

examples.chinese: 我们从学校到公园(我們從學校到公園)。
examples.english: We go from the school to the park.

examples.chinese: 从春季到夏季(從春季到夏季)，花儿都在盛开(花兒都在盛開)。
examples.pinyin: Cóng chūnjì dào xiàjì, huā'ér dōu zài shèngkāi.
examples.english: From spring to summer, flowers are in full bloom.

examples.chinese: 他的工作时间是从早上九点到晚上五点(他的工作時間是從早上九點到晚上五點)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de gōngzuò shíjiān shì cóng zǎoshang jiǔ diǎn dào wǎnshang wǔ diǎn.
examples.english: His working hours are from 9 AM to 5 PM.
